_comment: Recommended pi-blackhole config for openai-codex/gpt-5.5 daily driver; auto compaction; Cursor primary workers; non-Cursor fallbacks.

observerModel._comment: Cursor primary: very cheap/fast thinking-capable model for frequent observation extraction.

observerFallbackModels._comment: Non-Cursor fallback for observer; cheap/fast Google model.
Second non-Cursor fallback for observer on another provider.

reflectorModel._comment: Cursor primary: Cursor default/auto model for synthesis/reflection. Thinking is off because cursor/default is listed as non-thinking.

reflectorFallbackModels._comment: Non-Cursor fallback for reflector; strong long-context reasoning.
Second non-Cursor fallback for reflector on another provider.

dropperModel._comment: Cursor primary: cheap mini model with low thinking for memory pruning/judgment.

dropperFallbackModels._comment: Second non-Cursor fallback for dropper on another provider.

_notes: Daily driver/session model: openai-codex/gpt-5.5, context window from pi --list-models: 272K.
compactAfterTokens: 180000 = 66.2% of 272K; formula target was 272000 * 0.65 = 176800, rounded to high preset.
tailBehavior is pi-default only: blackhole still owns compaction, but Pi's gentler recent-tail cut is used.
Cursor primary workers all have 200K context. Reflector primary is cursor/default (Cursor default/auto). The largest worker input cap is 160K = 80.0% of the Cursor worker context.
Fallbacks are deliberately non-Cursor so primary and fallback providers differ.
Final emergency fallback remains the session model because sessionFallback is true: openai-codex/gpt-5.5.
